old_claim.note: Maritime Pilot Books provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Pilgrimage Route Services and Maritime Pilot Books; Pilgrimage route services are broader than sea-route pilot books and should not depend directly on maritime sailing directions.

source_shape.source_locator: Research guide identifies portolans, rutters, sea-atlases, sailing directions, and pilot books as written aids for navigation, with the earliest written books of sailing directions focused on the Mediterranean.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Pilgrimage route services are broader than sea-route pilot books and should not depend directly on maritime sailing directions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Pilgrimage Route Services claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Maritime Pilot Books as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Pilgrimage Route Services treated Maritime Pilot Books as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Pilgrimage Route Services no longer directly depends on Maritime Pilot Books; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: pilgrimage_route_services must not directly depend on maritime_pilot_books unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows pilgrimage_route_services is intentionally scoped only to cases that require maritime_pilot_books.
The graph splits pilgrimage_route_services and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on maritime_pilot_books.
A stronger source demonstrates that maritime_pilot_books is chronologically and causally necessary for pilgrimage_route_services.
short_reason: Pilgrimage route services are broader than sea-route pilot books and should not depend directly on maritime sailing directions.
